questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the given circuit diagram, find the heat generated on closing the switch S. (Initially the capacitor of capacitance C is uncharged)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{i}}=\frac{1}{2} \frac{6}{5} C \mathrm{~V}^2 ....(i)\\
& \mathrm{q}_{\mathrm{i}}=\frac{6}{5} \mathrm{CV}....(ii) \\
& \mathrm{q}_{\mathrm{f}}=\frac{11}{5} \mathrm{CV}....(iii) \\
& \mathrm{U}_{\mathrm{f}}=\left(\frac{1}{2} \frac{6}{5} C V^2+\frac{1}{2} C V^2\right)
\end{aligned}$
Charge flown from battery = CV
Work done $=\mathrm{CV}^2$
Heat produced $\Delta \mathrm{H}=\Delta \mathrm{U}+\Delta \mathrm{W}$
$=\left[\left(\frac{1}{2} \frac{6}{5} C V^2+\frac{1}{2} C V^2\right)-\frac{1}{2} \frac{6}{5} C V^2\right]-C V^2=-\frac{1}{2} C V^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Three similar plates are arranged as shown in the figure. If area of each plate is $A$ and a charge $+Q$ is given to middle plate then potential of middle plate is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & E_1=\frac{\sigma}{\epsilon_0}=\frac{Q}{2 A \epsilon_0} \\ & V=E_1 d=\frac{Q d}{2 A \epsilon_0}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $C$ be the capacitance of a capacitor discharging through a resistor $R$. Suppose $t_1$ is the time taken for the energy stored in the capacitor to reduce to half its initial value and $t_2$ is the time taken for the charge to reduce to one-fourth its initial value. Then the ratio $t_1 / t_2$ will be
questions.solution:
$U=\frac{1}{2} \frac{q^2}{C}=\frac{1}{2 C}\left(q_0 e^{-t / T}\right)^2=\frac{q_0^2}{2 C} e^{-2 t / T}(where \quad\tau=\mathrm{CR})$ 
$U=U_1 e^{-2 t_1 / \tau}$
$\frac{1}{2} U_1=U_1 e^{-2 t_1 / \tau} ; \frac{1}{2}=e^{-2 t_1 / \tau} \Rightarrow t_1=\frac{T}{2} \ln 2$

Now, $\quad q=q_0 e^{-t / T}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{4} \mathrm{q}_{\mathrm{o}}=\mathrm{q}_{\mathrm{o}} \mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{t} / 2 \mathrm{~T}} \\ & \mathrm{t}_2=\mathrm{T} \ln 4=2 T \ln 2 \\ \therefore \quad & \frac{\mathrm{t}_1}{\mathrm{t}_2}=\frac{1}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The left plate of the capacitor shown in the figure above carries a charge $+Q$ while the right plate is uncharged at $t=0$. The total charge on the right plate after closing the switch will be
questions.solution: Electric field between the capacitor plates $=\frac{\sigma_1}{2 \varepsilon_0}+\frac{\left(-\sigma_2\right)}{2 \varepsilon_0}$ $\mathrm{E}=\frac{\mathrm{Q}+\mathrm{X}}{2 \mathrm{~A} \varepsilon_0}+\frac{\mathrm{X}}{2 \mathrm{~A} \varepsilon_0}=\frac{1}{2 \mathrm{~A} \varepsilon_0}[\mathrm{Q}+2 \mathrm{x}]$ $\Rightarrow$ Potential different $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{d}}=\frac{d}{2 A \varepsilon_0}[Q+2 x]=\varepsilon$ $\Rightarrow \varepsilon=\frac{\mathrm{Q}+2 \mathrm{x}}{2 \mathrm{C}}$ $\Rightarrow-\mathrm{x}=\frac{\mathrm{Q}}{2}-\mathrm{C} \varepsilon$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: A dielectric slab of thickness ' $d$ ' is inserted in the parallel plate capacitor whose negative plate is at $\mathrm{x}=0$ and positive plate is at $\mathrm{x}=3 \mathrm{~d}$. The slab is equidistant from the plates. The capacitor is given some charge. As $x$ goes from 0 to 3d .
options: the magnitude of the electric field remains the same
the direction of the electric field remains the same
the electric potential increases first, then decreases and again increases
questions.solution: (1) Direction remain same (2) Potential continuously increase
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The switch in circuit shifts from 1 to 2 when $V_C\gt2 \mathrm{~V} / 3$ and goes back to 1 from 2 when $V_C \lt V / 3$. The voltmeter reads voltage as plotted. What is the period T of the wave form in terms of R and C ?
questions.solution:
During time ' $\mathrm{t}{ }_2$ ' capacitor is discharging with the help of resistor ' R '
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \mathrm{q}=\mathrm{q}_0 \mathrm{e}^{-t / \mathrm{RC}} \\
& \mathrm{~V}=\mathrm{V}_0 \mathrm{e}^{-t / R C} \quad[\because \mathrm{Q}=\mathrm{CV}]
\end{aligned}$
As $\mathrm{V}_0=\frac{2 \mathrm{~V}}{3} ; \mathrm{V}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{3}$
$\mathrm{t}_2=\mathrm{RC} \ln 2$
During time ' $\mathrm{t}_1$ ' capacitor is charging with the help of battery.
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \mathrm{q}=\mathrm{q}_0\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-t / R C}\right) \text { or } \mathrm{V}=\mathrm{V}_0\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-\mathrm{t} / \mathrm{RC}}\right) \\
& \text { as } \mathrm{V}_0=\frac{2 \mathrm{~V}}{3} ; \mathrm{V}=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{3} \\
& \mathrm{t}_1=\mathrm{RC} \ln 2 \\
& \mathrm{~T}=\mathrm{t}_1+\mathrm{t}_2=2 \mathrm{RC} \ln 2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
